questions.question: What is the compression-to-ventilation ratio for 2-rescuer ADULT CPR without an advanced airway?
questions.rationale: The adult ratio remains 30:2 regardless of the number of rescuers. Only child and infant CPR change to 15:2 when a second rescuer is present.

questions.question: How often should the compressor role be rotated during CPR?
questions.rationale: Switching about every 2 minutes (every 5 cycles) prevents the decline in compression depth that fatigue causes, and the switch should take under 10 seconds.

questions.question: Once an advanced airway is in place during adult CPR, how are compressions and ventilations delivered?
options: B. Continuous compressions with 1 breath every 6 seconds
C. Stop compressions during each breath
questions.rationale: With an advanced airway, rescuers stop pausing for breaths. They give continuous compressions and one breath every 6 seconds (about 10/min), maximizing the compression fraction.

questions.question: What is closed-loop communication?
options: A. Speaking only to the team leader
B. Giving an order, having it repeated back, and confirming completion
C. Remaining silent to avoid distraction
questions.rationale: Closed-loop communication means the receiver repeats the order back and reports when it is done, so the leader knows tasks are understood and completed.

questions.question: When is the ideal time to perform a compressor switch to minimize interruption?
options: A. In the middle of a compression cycle
B. During the AED rhythm analysis pause
questions.rationale: Timing the switch to the AED rhythm-analysis pause uses a moment when compressions are already paused, so no additional interruption is added.

questions.question: Which of the following is an element of effective team dynamics?
options: D. Multiple leaders giving conflicting orders
questions.rationale: Clear roles and responsibilities is a core element of team dynamics, along with closed-loop communication, knowledge sharing, mutual respect, and constructive intervention.

questions.question: A team member notices the compressions look too shallow. What should they do?
options: A. Say nothing to avoid conflict
B. Use constructive intervention and respectfully alert the team
D. Wait until the code is over to mention it
questions.rationale: Constructive intervention means respectfully speaking up about a safety issue in real time, allowing correction that benefits the patient.

questions.question: Why does the adult ratio remain 30:2 even with multiple rescuers?
options: A. Because adults need fewer breaths than children
B. Because pediatric arrests are more often respiratory, so children benefit from more frequent ventilation at 15:2
C. Because adults cannot tolerate breaths
D. There is no physiological reason
questions.rationale: Pediatric arrests are frequently respiratory in origin, so children and infants receive more frequent ventilation (15:2 with two rescuers). Adult arrests are more often cardiac, so 30:2 is retained.

questions.question: With an advanced airway in place, approximately how many breaths per minute should be given?
questions.rationale: One breath every 6 seconds equals about 10 breaths per minute, delivered without interrupting continuous compressions.

questions.question: A two-rescuer team is doing adult CPR. Both stop and look at each other, each thinking the other is compressing. What team dynamics failure is this?
options: B. Unclear roles and lack of closed-loop communication
questions.rationale: This is a failure of clear role assignment and communication. Explicit roles and closed-loop confirmation prevent the dangerous gap where no one is compressing.

questions.question: What is the maximum time a compressor switch should take?
questions.rationale: Like all interruptions, a compressor switch should take less than 10 seconds so perfusion is minimally affected.

questions.question: The team leader's primary responsibility during resuscitation is to:
options: B. Coordinate the team, assign tasks, and monitor CPR quality
D. Document the event and nothing else
questions.rationale: The leader maintains situational awareness, assigns and monitors tasks, ensures high-quality CPR, and adapts the plan — rather than getting fixed on one hands-on task.

questions.question: Which practice best minimizes the post-shock pause?
options: A. Checking the pulse for 30 seconds after the shock
B. Resuming chest compressions immediately after the shock is delivered
C. Waiting to see if the patient wakes up
D. Reanalyzing the rhythm before compressions
questions.rationale: Compressions should resume immediately after a shock without a pulse or rhythm check, because early post-shock compressions support perfusion while the heart recovers.

questions.question: In 2-rescuer adult CPR without an advanced airway, how are the 2 breaths delivered?
options: A. During ongoing compressions without pausing
B. During a brief pause after each set of 30 compressions
D. Only once at the start
questions.rationale: Without an advanced airway, the compressor pauses briefly after 30 compressions while the second rescuer delivers 2 breaths, then compressions resume immediately.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly pairs a scenario with its ratio?
questions.rationale: Two-rescuer child (and infant) CPR uses 15:2. Adults stay at 30:2, and single-rescuer child CPR is 30:2.

questions.question: Why should ventilation be limited to about 10 breaths/min with an advanced airway?
options: B. To avoid over-ventilation, which raises intrathoracic pressure and reduces cardiac output
C. Because more breaths are impossible
D. To allow the compressor to rest
questions.rationale: Excessive ventilation increases intrathoracic pressure and impairs venous return and cardiac output, so a controlled rate of about 10/min is used with an advanced airway.

questions.question: A well-functioning team assigns a member to watch and call out CPR quality metrics. This best reflects which element?
questions.rationale: Monitoring and calling out metrics supports reassessment/summarizing and knowledge sharing, helping the team maintain high-quality CPR and adjust as needed.

questions.question: During 2-rescuer adult CPR, the AED advises 'no shock' and the patient remains pulseless. What should the team do?
options: B. Immediately resume high-quality compressions and continue the 2-minute cycles
questions.rationale: A 'no shock advised' with no pulse means resume compressions immediately and continue CPR in 2-minute cycles until the next analysis or return of a pulse.
